channel.title: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: ‘We watched them die before our eyes’: Sudan health workers helpless amid medical shortages
item.description: As violence forces tens of thousands to flee Sudan’s South Kordofan state, doctors in a key maternity hospital are facing impossible choices – with too few supplies, too many patients, and lives slipping away.

item.title: Deminers race to keep up with military technology
item.description: In conflict zones where new technologies are making landmines more dangerous, deminers must innovate at the same pace to avoid being left behind, a leading UN mines expert has told UN News.

item.title: Ugandan Farmers Sue EACOP in London in Last Minute Effort to Stop Crude Oil Pipeline
item.description: Environmental activists and farmer groups opposed to the construction of the East African Crude Oil Pipeline (EACOP), the world’s longest heated oil pipeline, are mounting a last-ditch legal effort meant to stop its construction in a suit they plan to have filed in London, UK, believing that it stands a chance to stop the controversial […]

item.title: Despite a New Wave of Infections, No Mask or Vaccine Mandates
item.description: A fifth wave of a new Covid 19 variant BA.2, followed by a surge in infections, is threatening to undermine the safety of New York city (NYC) which was gradually returning to normal after a prolonged pandemic shutdown. As a result, the City went on “high Covid alert.” But NYC Mayor Eric Adams has assured […]

item.title: New York City Cracks Down on Homeless People Cluttering Streets & Subways
item.description: Faced with a growing problem of homeless people living and sleeping in park benches and on subway trains, New York city (NYC) authorities are physically moving them out—mostly under protest– to some 150 encampments or public shelters. A cleanup crew removed all of their belongings lying scattered in a “miniature tent city” across from Tompkins […]

item.title: The Inter-American Development Bank Invest Talks Growth– but Ignores People Bearing the Cost
item.description: In Asunción, Paraguay last month, finance ministers, central bank presidents, and private sector leaders gathered for the Inter-American Development Bank’s (IDB) Annual Meetings to talk about growth. In a session titled “Seizing Opportunities, Stimulating Growth” hosted by IDB Invest, the bank’s private sector institution, they discussed how investment and innovation could strengthen agribusiness and food […]

item.title: Iran War: What African Countries Can do to Get Through the Crisis and Emerge in a Better Place
item.description: By Easter 2026 it was still not clear when – or how – the war initiated by Israel and the US against Iran would end. But what was already clear was that it would harm Africa in a number of ways. Firstly, it would adversely affect the global supply and prices of oil and gas, […]
